title: Bus Ticket Inquiry
opening: Good morning! Where are you headed today?

t1.prompt: Good morning! Where are you headed today?
t1.promptTranslation: Good morning! [ˈɡʊd ˈmɔːrnɪŋ!] Where are you headed today? (Idiomatic 'headed' means 'going', a common informal opening to ask for destination).

t-repeat.prompt: I'm sorry, I didn't quite catch that. Could you please rephrase or speak a little clearer?
t-repeat.promptTranslation: I'm sorry, I didn't quite catch that. (Common idiom for 'I didn't hear/understand'). Could you please rephrase or speak a little clearer?

t2.prompt: Alright, for the City Centre, you'll want the number 22 bus. It leaves from Stand B. A single fare is £2.80. The journey usually takes about 30 minutes, roughly 8 stops, alighting at 'Grand Avenue'. What's your preference regarding the ticket?
t2.promptTranslation: Alright, for the City Centre, you'll want the number 22 bus. (Natural phrasing 'you'll want' implies recommendation). It leaves from Stand B. A single fare is £2.80. The journey usually takes about 30 minutes, roughly 8 stops, alighting at 'Grand Avenue'. (Formal verb 'alight' for disembarking, used in public transport announcements). What's your preference regarding the ticket? (Common way to ask for choice).

t3.prompt: Right, there are a few ways to get around. Whereabouts are you hoping to reach, or is there a particular landmark you're aiming for? We can sort out the quickest route for you.
t3.promptTranslation: Right, there are a few ways to get around. (Common informal agreement/acknowledgement). Whereabouts are you hoping to reach, or is there a particular landmark you're aiming for? We can sort out the quickest route for you. ('Sort out' is idiomatic for 'arrange/figure out').

t4_standard.prompt: Excellent choice. One single fare coming right up. That'll be £2.80. Are you paying by cash or card?
t4_standard.promptTranslation: Excellent choice. One single fare coming right up. (Idiom: 'coming right up' means preparing immediately). That'll be £2.80. Are you paying by cash or card?

t6.prompt: If you're looking for the quickest way to the city centre, the express bus service, route 100, is your best bet. It runs every 15 minutes from Stand C and gets you there in about 20 minutes, non-stop. A single fare is £3.50. Does that sound suitable?
t6.promptTranslation: If you're looking for the quickest way to the city centre, the express bus service, route 100, is your best bet. (Idiom: 'best bet' means best option). It runs every 15 minutes from Stand C and gets you there in about 20 minutes, non-stop. A single fare is £3.50. Does that sound suitable? (Polite check for agreement).

t4_express.prompt: Right then, one express single fare for £3.50. Cash or card?
t4_express.promptTranslation: Right then, (Common conversational filler, means 'okay then') one express single fare for £3.50. Cash or card?

t-end.prompt: You're all set! Have a pleasant journey and enjoy your day!
t-end.promptTranslation: You're all set! (Idiom: 'all set' means ready to go/finished). Have a pleasant journey and enjoy your day! (Standard polite closing).
